tldr: Appknox specialises in mobile-app binary security testing, while SonarQube is a SAST and code-quality platform across many languages with a free Community edition. The migration moves you from binary-focused mobile scans to source-code static analysis, so coverage will shift rather than be identical.
why_switch: Standardise on a SAST and quality platform across all repositories, not just mobile
Use the OSS Community edition to control costs
Catch issues at commit time rather than post-build
what_to_export: List of scanned mobile apps and their identifiers
Open and historical findings with severity and status
export_method: Export each Appknox project's findings as report files (PDF or JSON) and archive them with the date of issue for audit retention. Capture suppression decisions and any risk-accepted items in a structured list so they can be re-applied as SonarQube issue resolutions. Document the list of mobile apps mapped to their source repositories — SonarQube needs the source, not the binary. Do not attempt to migrate raw scan engines or binary artifacts; these are tool-specific.
import_method: Install SonarQube (self-hosted Community/Developer/Enterprise or SonarQube Cloud) and create a project per repository that previously fed Appknox. Configure the build pipeline to run the SonarQube scanner against the source on every PR. Re-apply suppressions as SonarQube issue resolutions (False Positive / Won't Fix) so historical decisions carry forward. Set quality gates that cover the severities your compliance program requires, and verify that the language analyzers cover your mobile codebases (Java, Kotlin, Swift, Objective-C). Run both tools in parallel until each repository's quality gate is tuned.
gotchas: SonarQube scans source code; binary-only mobile checks Appknox performed may not have an equivalent
Re-applying suppressions is manual and easy to forget — script it where possible
Self-hosted SonarQube requires infrastructure, upgrades, and DB maintenance
Quality gates fail builds by default — tune thresholds before turning on PR blocking
Some mobile-specific rules require the paid edition; verify license needs
Historical Appknox reports remain in Appknox unless exported in advance

email.subject: Mobile and code security checks are moving to SonarQube
email.body:
Hi team,

We are moving our application security and code-quality checks from Appknox to SonarQube over the next 14 days. SonarQube will run on source code in CI rather than on built binaries, so findings will appear at PR time. Both tools will run in parallel while we tune the quality gates. Please flag any active Appknox finding that you want carried over.

Thanks for your patience.

Your customer
